author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
The Beginning of Faith is God’s Gift.
title: The Beginning of Faith is God’s Gift.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Finally, also, in what follows this testimony, the apostle gives thanks to God on behalf of those who have believed;—not, certainly, because the gospel has been declared to them, but because they have believed.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For he says, “In whom also after ye had heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation; in whom also, after that ye believed, ye were sealed with the Holy Spirit of promise, which is the pledge of our inheritance, to the redemption of the purchased possession unto the praise of His glory.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Wherefore I also, after I had heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and with reference to all the saints, cease not to give thanks for you.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Their faith was new and recent on the preaching of the gospel to them, which faith when he hears of, the apostle gives thanks to God on their behalf.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: If he were to give thanks to man for that which he might either think or know that man had not given, it would be called a flattery or a mockery, rather than a giving of thanks.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “Do not err, for God is not mocked;” for His gift is also the beginning of faith, unless the apostolic giving of thanks be rightly judged to be either mistaken or fallacious.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Does that not appear as the beginning of the faith of the Thessalonians, for which, nevertheless, the same apostle gives thanks to God when he says, “For this cause also we thank God without ceasing, because when ye had received from us the word of the hearing of God, ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth the word of God, which effectually worketh in you and which ye believed”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What is that for which he here gives thanks to God?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Assuredly it is a vain and idle thing if He to whom he gives thanks did not Himself do the thing.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But, since this is not a vain and idle thing, certainly God, to whom he gave thanks concerning this work, Himself did it; that when they had received the word of the hearing of God, they received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth the word of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: God, therefore, worketh in the hearts of men with that calling according to His purpose, of which we have spoken a great deal, that they should not hear the gospel in vain, but when they heard it, should be converted and believe, receiving it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth the word of God.
